Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents a social media post, encompassing content, scheduling, and channel-specific configurations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

workspaceId.description: Reference to Workspace. (Relationship: Workspace 1:N Post). The agency/client owning the post.

createdByUid.description: User ID of the user who created the post.

source.description: Source of the content: manual or AI-generated.

status.description: Status of the post: draft, scheduled, publishing, published, failed.

hashtags.description: Array of hashtags associated with the post.

callToAction.description: Call to action text for the post.

aiConfig.description: Configuration details for AI-generated content (if applicable).

media.description: Information about the media associated with the post.

channels.description: Array of channels where the post will be published.

metrics.description: Metrics for the post (impressions, likes, comments, etc.).

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the post was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the post was last updated.

deletedAt.description: Timestamp of when the post was deleted (if applicable).

Workspace.title: Workspace
Workspace.description: Represents a workspace (agency/client) in the system, enabling multi-tenant support.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Workspace entity.

plan.description: Subscription plan of the workspace (basic, pro, expert).

ownerUid.description: User ID of the workspace owner.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the workspace was created.

billingCustomerId.description: Reference to billing information stored in the payment provider (e.g., Stripe).

WorkspaceUser.title: WorkspaceUser
WorkspaceUser.description: Represents a user's membership in a workspace, defining their role and access level.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkspaceUser entity.

workspaceId.description: Reference to Workspace. (Relationship: Workspace 1:N WorkspaceUser). ID of the workspace the user is a member of.

role.description: Role of the user in the workspace (owner, admin, member).

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the user joined the workspace.

SocialAccount.title: SocialAccount
SocialAccount.description: Represents a social media account connected to the system, storing display information but NOT sensitive tokens.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialAccount entity.

workspaceId.description: Reference to Workspace. (Relationship: Workspace 1:N SocialAccount). ID of the workspace the social account belongs to.

displayName.description: Display name of the social account.

avatarUrl.description: URL of the social account's avatar (profile picture).

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the social account was connected.

connected.description: Indicates whether the social account is currently connected.

definition.description: Represents a workspace (agency/client) in the system.  Authorization is based on the 'ownerUid' field.
Represents a user's membership in a workspace, defining their role and access level.

params.name: membershipId
params.description: Unique identifier for the workspace membership.

definition.description: Represents a social media post. Includes denormalized 'workspaceId' for authorization independence.
Represents a social media account connected to the system. Includes denormalized 'workspaceId' for authorization independence.

params.name: accountId
params.description: Unique identifier for the social account.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support ViralinkAI's multi-tenant SaaS platform, focusing on secure access control, scalability, and data integrity. It leverages denormalization and structural segregation to simplify security rules and ensure authorization independence.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   The `posts` and `socialAccounts` collections include the `workspaceId` field. This denormalization allows security rules to validate workspace access without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent `workspaces` documents.
*   `workspaceUsers` serves as a membership map to define user roles within workspaces. This supports collaborative access control without relying on complex hierarchical lookups.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   Each collection is designed to store data with a homogeneous security posture. For example, all documents within the `posts` collection share the same access control requirements (workspace-based access).
*   Sensitive data, such as social media tokens, are NOT stored directly in the `socialAccounts` collection. This ensures that client-side code cannot access these tokens.

**Access Modeling:**

*   The `workspaces/{workspaceId}` collection uses a path-based approach for workspace data, allowing clear ownership and access control based on the `ownerUid` field.
*   The `workspaceUsers` collection implements a membership map, defining user roles within each workspace. This enables collaborative access control with well-defined roles (owner, admin, member).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The inclusion of `workspaceId` in the `posts` and `socialAccounts` documents, combined with security rules that enforce `workspaceId` validation, ensures that list operations are secure and efficient.
*   The structure supports secure list operations because the rules can filter based on the denormalized `workspaceId` without needing to fetch additional documents or perform complex calculations.
*   The authorization rules will be database-centric (DBAC), using the `workspaceUsers` collection as the source of truth for user authorization.

**Invariants:**

*   Timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `deletedAt`) are included in entities to track data modification history and support data integrity.
*   The use of explicit state modeling (`status` field in `posts`) provides a clear and predictable way to track the lifecycle of posts.
